body.title: Cost cap skips flat-rate sources; separate higher turn cap for Ollama Pro
body.result: Separate turn/cost caps by billing type: flat-rate MAX_TURNS_FLAT_RATE=80 (no cost cap), usage_based COST_CAP_USD=$3
body.intention: I intend that flat-rate sources (ollama_cloud) are governed by a turn cap not a dollar cap, and that usage-based sources (openrouter) keep the $3 cost cap.
body.description:
COST_CAP_USD=$3 in ToolLoop fires for all sources including flat-rate Ollama Pro where real cost is $0. Devstral ran 30 turns at $0 real cost but was on the verge of COST_EXCEEDED. Wrong constraint applied to wrong billing type.

Fix: in ToolLoop.run(), check source billing_type before applying cost cap. For flat_rate sources: use a higher turn cap (e.g. MAX_TURNS_FLAT_RATE=50) and skip the cost accumulation exit. For usage_based: keep COST_CAP_USD=$3 as now. Source billing_type is available via the models_registry or can be inferred from model_override by checking OllamaCloudSource.

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py, possibly devices/dicksimnel/device.py
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** IN: split turn/cost caps by billing type. OUT: changing cascade logic, routing rules.
**Completion criteria:** devstral run on an S-size ticket completes with DONE: within 50 turns; haiku run still terminates at $3 cost cap. Both verified by test or live run.
